data.title: Singtel: Shares Represent About 1.2% of Bharti Airtel's Total Number of Equity Shares
Merida Industry May See Weaker-Than-Expected Demand in China — Market Talk
data.description: Merida Industry may see weaker-than-expected demand in China, Daiwa Capital Markets analysts say in a research report. According to the Taiwanese bicycle manufacturer, its China operations in 2025 will be under pressure from high inventory levels and a slowdown in shipments, the analysts note. Merida Industry has cut its 2025 China shipment forecast to 700,000-800,000 units from 800,000-1,000,000 units previously. The company also expects total revenue to decline 3%-5% this year, mainly owing to a 35%-40% revenue decrease in China. The brokerage cuts its 2025-2026 EPS forecasts for Merida Industry by 16%-21%. It lowers the stock's target price to NT$130.00 from NT$150.00 with an unchanged hold rating. Shares are 6.5% lower at NT$123.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Japan Industrial Output Revised Higher
data.description:
Japan’s industrial production increased by 0.2% month-over-month in March 2025, beating flash data of a 1.1% decline.

The latest result followed a 2.3% rise in the previous month, marking the second straight month of growth.

Output was mainly higher for production machinery (7.1% vs 8.3% in February), transport equipment excluding motor vehicles (6.6% vs -9.0%), and chemicals (12.5% vs -1.0%).

On a yearly basis, industrial output grew by 1.0%, picking up from a 0.1% rise in February and marking the third consecutive annual increase.

data.title: Turkey - Factors To Watch on May 16
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Friday.

The lira was quoted at 38.8775 against the U.S. dollar at 0400 GMT, weaker than its close of 38.7305 on Thursday.

The main BIST 100 share index closed 1.65% lower at 9,541.30 points on Thursday.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asian stocks were ending a strong week on a softer note on Friday as the euphoria over U.S.-China trade talks faded, while revived bets for policy easing in the United States sparked a rally in beaten-down bond markets.

RUSSIA-UKRAINE TALKS

Russian and Ukrainian delegations are set to hold technical talks in Istanbul, marking the first direct discussions between the warring sides in three years after Russian President Vladimir Putin rejected a face-to-face meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy.

Senior U.S. and Turkish officials will join the talks, which aim for a potential ceasefire to the deadliest conflict in Europe since World War Two.

A tripartite meeting between the Ukrainian presidential administration chief Andriy Yermak, Turkish officials including Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan, and U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio is expected.

This will be followed by a three-way meeting between Russian, Ukrainian, and Turkish delegations, also attended by Fidan.

UKRAINE BRIEFING

Ukrainian delegation is expected to brief the media after talks with Russia.

IRAN NUCLEAR TALKS

Iran will hold talks in Istanbul with European parties regarding their now-moribund 2015 nuclear deal, according to Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi.

ERDOGAN SCHEDULE

President Tayyip Erdogan will attend the 6th summit of the European Political Community in Albania and a youth event in Istanbul.

VICE PRESIDENT YILMAZ

Vice President Cevdet Yılmaz will attend the launch event of the Cyprus Tourism Master Plan in Istanbul. (1130)

CENTRAL BANK SURVEY

The Central Bank will release the results of its Market Participants Survey for May. (0700)

ECONOMIC DATA

Turkish Statistical Institute (TUIK) will release housing sales statistics for April and labour force statistics for Q1. (0700)

For other related news, double-click on:

data.title: Australia's GPT Group rises on partnership deal with QuadReal
data.description:
** Shares of GPT Group rise 2.8% to A$4.76, their biggest intraday percentage gain since April 10

** The retail assets operator and QuadReal announced on Thursday a A$1 billion ($642.40 million) Australian logistics partnership

** The partnership will help establish a joint venture, which will serve as the primary vehicle for QuadReal to scale its Australian logistics investments, and will be operated and managed by GPT, adds co

** QuadReal's total commitment for an 80% stake in the partnership is A$400 million, says co

** Stock up 6% this year, as of last close

data.title: Sipa Resources Undertakes Heritage Clearance Survey on South Australia Gold Projects; Shares Down 8%
data.description:
Sipa Resources said its exploration team at the Tunkillia North and Nuckulla Hill gold projects in South Australia will undertake a heritage clearance survey with representatives of the Gawler Ranges Aboriginal, ahead of drilling on both projects, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The survey is expected to take around five days, with the formal archaeologist's report expected to be completed in around a month.

The program for environmental protection and rehabilitation for the drilling programs has been submitted to South Australia's Department for Energy and Mining.

The company's shares tumbled 8% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 10:10 a.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was down 0.3% to 82289, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 fell 0.2% to 25000, on likely profit-booking after the indexes hit a seven-month high on Thursday on hopes of a U.S.-India trade deal.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee declined 0.1% versus the U.S. dollar to 85.6425, pressured by dollar outflows likely related to a large equity block deal.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN067934G=CC) was quoted at 103.665 rupees, with the yield down 1 bp at 6.2661%, tracking a fall in U.S. Treasury yields.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was down 1 basis point at 5.62%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) dipped 3 bps to 5.64%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.75%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.68%

data.title: Norwood Systems Raises AU$500,000 Via Placement; Shares Hit 52-Week Low
data.description:
Norwood Systems raised AU$500,000 via an over-subscribed placement to sophisticated and professional investors through an issue of 26.3 million fully paid ordinary shares at AU$0.019 per share, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The company will use the funds for strategic market expansion efforts, accelerated product development, customer experience enhancements, operational efficiency improvements, a partial repayment of debt, as well as the pursuit of strategic partnerships, per the filing.

Norwood Systems' shares fell nearly 13% in recent trading on Friday, earlier hitting a 52-week low.

data.title: Larsen & Toubro Bumps Up Stake in E2E Networks
data.description:
Engineering and infrastructure company Larsen & Toubro has completed the acquisition of an additional 2.98% stake in E2E Networks, taking its total shareholding to 18.99%, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The company, on Thursday, acquired 595,916 equity shares of E2E Networks through secondary acquisition from the promoter of E2E Networks.

data.title: Hankyu Hanshin Posts Flat Profit in Fiscal 2025
data.description:
Hankyu Hanshin Holdings posted a profit attributable to owners of 67.4 billion yen for fiscal 2025, compared to 67.8 billion yen a year earlier.

The transport company's earnings per share came in at 281.45 yen, against 281.57 yen a year ago, according to a Tokyo bourse filing on May 14.

Operating revenues jumped 11% to 1.11 trillion yen from 997.6 billion yen a year earlier.

The company declared a final dividend of 30 yen per share for fiscal 2025, payable from June 18.

For fiscal 2026, Hankyu Hanshin expects an attributable profit of 75 billion yen, or 315.57 yen per share, while operating revenues are forecast to reach 1.19 trillion yen.

data.title: India's Neuland Labs falls on Q4 profit slump
data.description:
** Shares of Neuland Labs down 6% at 11,154 rupees

** Q4 profit slumped 59%, hurt by specialty and custom manufacturing segment performance

** NEUL shares down ~14% YTD vs 8% drop in Nifty pharma index

data.title: Canadian oil producer Strathcona initiates $4.25 billion takeover bid for MEG Energy
data.description:
Canadian oil and gas producer Strathcona said late Thursday it plans to launch a C$5.93 billion ($4.25 billion) takeover bid for peer MEG Energy , aiming to create the country’s fifth-largest oil producer.

Strathcona will offer to buy all of the issued and outstanding common shares of MEG Energy for 0.62 of a common share of Strathcona and C$4.10 in cash per MEG Share.

The offer represents a total consideration of C$23.27 per MEG share, Strathcona said, which is a premium of 9.3% to MEG Energy's last closing price. A Reuters calculation shows that will value MEG at about C$5.93 billion.

After completion of the offer, Strathcona expects to have about $1.5 billion in net debt.

On April 28, the company made a formal proposal to MEG Energy's board of directors. But on May 13, 2025, the MEG Board said it was not interested in pursuing a combination with Strathcona.

($1 = 1.3944 Canadian dollars)

data.title: Jiangsu Pharmaceutical to Present Study Results on Two Lymphoma Drugs at Global Conferences After Showing Positive Results
data.description:
Dizal Jiangsu Pharmaceutical will present the results of its studies on two lymphoma drugs in international academic conferences this year after the drugs showed positive results during the clinical trials, according to a Friday disclosure on the Shanghai bourse.

The drugs Golidocitinib and DZD8586, which both showed positive safety profiles at their clinical studies, will be presented at the American Society of Clinical Oncology's annual meeting this year.

The Chinese pharmaceutical company will also present the latest clinical data from eight studies in its hematologic oncology pipeline at the 2025 European Hematology Association Annual Congress and the 18th International Conference on Malignant Lymphoma in June.

data.title: Singtel: Subsidiary Pastel Sold 71M Bharti Airtel Shares for About S$2B
EMEA Morning Briefing: Futures Muted as Markets Await Trade Developments
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

Eurozone foreign trade, France jobless rate; trading updates for Richemont, Swiss Re

Opening Call:

European stock futures were flat after Asia stocks traded mixed. U.S. Treasurys were flat while the dollar fell. Gold fell while oil futures edged higher.

Equities:

European futures pointed to a muted open as investors further digested the impact of tariff developments.

"Consumers are slowing down and stretched," said Megan Horneman, chief investment officer at Verdence Capital Advisors.

"While inflation is a risk, I'm not worried about the type of inflation we got after COVID because I just don't think consumers will pay it and the companies will have to absorb that."

Forex:

Investors are looking ahead to potential developments on trade negotiations.

The prospect of more trade deal announcements is keeping some investors bullish on further market advances, according to Rick Gardner, chief investment officer at RGA Investments.

"It's still too early to say the worst is behind us, but with potential deals about to be announced, my expectation is that the markets will continue to push higher," Gardner said.

Bonds:

Volatility in U.S. Treasurys has been coming down, Neuberger Berman's Jeff Blazek and Erik Knutzen said.

U.S. Treasurys' spread over swap rates, an indicator of stress in the market, has tightened, the co-CIOs for multi-asset strategies said.

"Implied volatility indices have returned to more normal levels," they said.

Energy:

Oil futures edged higher on a possible technical recovery after they posted back-to-back losses overnight.

Thursday's oil-price selloff came after "President Trump indicated the U.S. could be close to a deal in which Iran agrees to forgo nuclear weapons," said NAB's Ray Attrill says in a commentary.

"Were there to be an agreement that results in the lifting of sanctions, Iran has the capability to pump about 4 million barrels of oil per day, up from around 1.4 million barrels currently," said the head of FX Research.

Metals:

Gold prices have been showing notable resilience, said Pepperstone's Quasar Elizundia. A key driver behind this resilience has been the latest U.S. CPI and PPI readings, which have given encouraging disinflationary signals, said the research strategist.

-

Copper edged lower amid cautious sentiment.

Recovery in the base-metals sector has faltered as optimism about a truce in the U.S.-led trade war fades, ANZ Research analysts said.

Base metals have recently ridden the resultant wave of positive market sentiment as tariff reductions lifted the global economic outlook and raised demand expectations.

-

Iron ore prices were lower. Meaningful growth in iron ore demand is unlikely until new construction activity lifts in China, ANZ Research said.

While the iron ore market faces short-term pressure, inventories-related pressure has eased, Jinyuan Futures analysts said, adding that investors should closely monitor potential near-term inventory level changes.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

Shipping Rates Rise as U.S.-China Trade Truce Drives Import Surge

The cost to ship a container of Chinese-made goods to the U.S. is jumping as importers race to pull cargo across the Pacific during a three-month trade-war truce.

Freight rates from China to the U.S. West Coast have risen about 8% this week as bookings have boomed, shipping executives and brokers in Singapore and London say. Ocean carriers are jacking up that rate by as much as 50% in the coming 10 days or so, they added. This means shipping a container from Shanghai to Los Angeles in the coming weeks could cost over $3,000 per twenty-foot equivalent unit, or TEU, the standard industry gauge.

Trump Administration Targets Europe's Digital Laws as a Threat to Basic Rights and U.S. Business

WASHINGTON-The Trump administration is ramping up attacks on Europe's digital rules, opening a new front in Washington's pressure campaign on longtime allies that combines assertions of defending free speech with efforts to protect commercial interests.

Much of the initiative is led by the State Department, which last week sent a request to its offices around Europe seeking "examples of government efforts to limit freedom of speech," according to a copy reviewed by The Wall Street Journal.

Trump Says U.S., Iran Close to Nuclear Deal

American negotiators for the first time pitched a nuclear proposal to their Iranian counterparts, according to people briefed on the matter, days before President Trump said the U.S. was close to an agreement on the matter on Thursday.

The U.S. issued its proposal last weekend during the fourth round of nuclear negotiations between the U.S. and Iran since April 12. After those talks, the U.S. said it was encouraged by Iran's openness to Washington's approach. The Iranians said the talks were "difficult but useful" in clarifying the differences.

He Calls Them Handsome, Attractive, Tough: Trump's Bromance With Arab Leaders

ABU DHABI-Three days in the Middle East have put President Trump's bromance diplomacy on full display.

"I like you too much," Trump told Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, later placing his hand on his heart as the two men bid farewell on the airport tarmac. Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa was "attractive" and "tough," Trump said after a surprise meeting in Riyadh with the former rebel leader who toppled Syria's longtime dictator in December. Trump said Qatar's ruling emir and his family were "tall, handsome guys."

Meta Is Delaying the Rollout of Its Flagship AI Model

Meta is delaying the rollout of a flagship AI model, prompting internal concerns about the direction of its multibillion-dollar AI investments, people familiar with the matter said.

Company engineers are struggling to significantly improve the capabilities of its "Behemoth" large-language model, leading to staff questions about whether improvements over prior versions are significant enough to justify public release, the people said.

Trump Presses Apple to Make iPhones in America. Wall Street Shrugs.

President Trump wants an American-made iPhone. Apple Chief Executive Tim Cook has no plans to deliver one any time soon.

Tension between the president and one of America's most valuable companies spilled into the open Thursday, when Trump chided Cook for the iPhone-maker's plans to shift final assembly for many U.S.-bound devices to India. Trump reiterated his demand that Apple bring manufacturing back to America.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

Expected Major Events for Friday

05:30/FRA: 1Q ILO Unemployment and Labour Market-related indicators

06:30/SWI: 1Q Industrial production

07:00/SVK: Mar New orders in industry

07:30/NED: Mar International trade

08:00/ITA: Apr CPI

09:00/ITA: Mar Foreign Trade EU

09:00/CRO: Apr CPI

09:00/EU: Mar Foreign trade

All times in GMT. Powered by Onclusive and Dow Jones.

Write to us at newsletters@dowjones.com

We offer an enhanced version of this briefing that is optimized for viewing on mobile devices and sent directly to your email inbox. If you would like to sign up, please go to https://newsplus.wsj.com/subscriptions.

This article is a text version of a Wall Street Journal newsletter published earlier today.

data.title: Trade-driven rally ends the week with a whimper
data.description:
A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Stella Qiu

The week started with a bang but by Friday the risk-on rally - supercharged by a China-U.S. trade truce - was wearing thin as traders grew wary that the rebound had overshot and that more twists and turns lie ahead in the trade saga.

Wall Street and European stock futures are little changed while Asian shares are mixed. Hong Kong's Hang Seng index fell 0.8%, weighed down by Alibaba's more than 5% tumble after its earnings failed to impress investors. Australian shares fared better, rising 0.7%.

The stock market now seems to be acting as if the tariff war never happened. The MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan (.MIAPJ0000PUS) is hovering just below a seven-month top. Even Chinese blue chips have recovered all of the losses since April 2 when President Donald Trump announced "reciprocal" tariffs - since put on hold - on the rest of the world.

Beaten-down bond markets cheered an unexpected fall in U.S. producer prices and a soft core retail sales print, prompting investors to nudge up their rate cut outlook for the Fed, pricing in a total of 56 basis points in rate cuts for this year, up from 49 bps.

Ten-year benchmark Treasury yields (US10YT=TWEB) fell 3 basis points to 4.424% on Friday, extending a 7 bps decline overnight.

Trump has meanwhile been busy in the past few days talking up deals in the Middle East, including a potential nuclear deal with Iran. Trump's comments that a deal was near sent oil prices tumbling 2% on Thursday.

The broader markets are keen, however, for progress in trade talks with China and details of more trade deals with other countries after an agreement with Britain.

Let's not forget tariffs are still a lot higher than before Trump began his crusade on trade, and the highest since the 1930s.

Walmart , the world's largest retailer, said it would have to start raising prices later this month due to the high cost of tariffs, pointing to more pain ahead for U.S. consumers.

Although the latest U.S. price data looked benign, it might be just a matter of time before the impact of tariffs starts to show up in the hard numbers that the Federal Reserve has said it needs to see before considering its response to trade-related uncertainties.

The economic calendars in Europe and the U.S. are a bit thin. U.S. releases on Friday include import prices for April and the University of Michigan consumer sentiment survey, which might be useful for gauging the impact of Trump's tariff manoeuvring.

Key developments that could influence markets on Friday:

- University of Michigan consumer sentiment survey

- U.S. import prices for April

data.title: BOJ's Nakamura calls for pause in rate hikes on US tariff uncertainty
data.description:
The Bank of Japan must hold off raising interest rates for the time being, its board member Toyoaki Nakamura said on Friday, warning of growing downward pressure on the economy from higher U.S. tariffs.

Nakamura, known as the most dovish member of the board, said the central bank must guide monetary policy "cautiously" with due attention to how extremely high uncertainty over U.S. trade policy could affect corporate and household activity.

"Japan's economy is facing mounting downward pressure," as steep U.S. tariffs, including on its mainstay automobile sector, could seriously hurt corporate profits, Nakamura said in a speech.

"Rushing to raise interest rates when growth is slowing could curb consumption and investment with a lag," said Nakamura, a former corporate executive whose five-year term at the board expires at the end of June.

data.title: SimCorner Expands New Unlimited Data eSIM Plans to Empower European Travelers
data.description:
The platform expands its Europe eSIM services by introducing unlimited data plans with instant activation and broader coverage as it aims to address the growing demand for seamless travel connectivity.

Victoria, Australia--(Newsfile Corp. - May 16, 2025) - SimCorner, a provider of international travel SIM cards and eSIMs, is officially launching its newly enhanced unlimited data eSIM plans for Europe. This strategic move aligns with the company's goal to respond to the increasing demand for travel connectivity.

SimCorner Expands New Unlimited Data eSIM Plans to Empower European Travelers

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/8814/251104_def04acfb44bba75_002full.jpg

Although SimCorner has previously offered European SIM services, the 2025 launch introduces an upgraded tier of unlimited eSIM data plans with broader country coverage and instant activation. These newly available plans are tailored for travelers who require uninterrupted data access across multiple European destinations, which makes them ideal for tourists, digital nomads, and business travelers.

This launch introduces unlimited data options across various durations, ranging from 5 days to 30 days and covering more than 35 European countries. With this, SimCorner's users are able to activate their eSIM instantly upon arrival during travel. This ensures zero downtime and maximum convenience for users.

With a mission to provide SIM and eSIM connections throughout Europe and other continents, SimCorner has developed tailored card plans to provide its users with reliable connectivity while traveling. This innovative approach to aid communication for European travelers has made the company a reliable platform in the industry.

The company remains committed to serving its users and working in accordance to empower them while traveling. This immediate activation and connectivity are delivered as the company dedicates its services to a higher telecom industry standard.

The expansion will allow the business to scale its operations while enhancing customer service to ultimately deliver greater value to its clients. The company has devised unlimited data eSIMs as part of this expansion after witnessing a hike among European travelers in 2025. This allows SimCorner to continue to meet the needs of its growing customer base.

As an innovative company that provides accessibility for European SIM Cards, the platform has a broad compatibility of networks that work with most eSIM-compatible smartphones, tablets, and smart devices. With accessible and reliable networks, SimCorner strives to deliver 4G and 5G networks for both fast and dependable connectivity across Europe.

With coverage spanning major destinations across Europe, including France, Germany, Italy, Spain, and the UK, the new plans provide a seamless travel experience. To help streamline the process more to make it easier and more efficient for clients, the company employs a simple QR code delivery for a straightforward installation of its services.

Advancements in the telecommunication industry by making reliable networks accessible have allowed SimCorner to deliver eSIM for travelers that enable users to stay connected through safe and secure online payments while traveling to Europe.

The platform takes pride in its user-centric approach by putting the user's needs at the forefront of the company's goals. The company provides services centered around users to help them achieve their individual goals, as it aims to simplify the process by offering various tailored plans to match users with their package preferences.

SimCorner attributes its success with its users to its team of highly professional and experienced customer support. Available at all times for its users, the team caters to assisting users in all Europe eSIM and Europe SIM Cards-related needs from the beginning of the process to help with setup or when users face troubleshooting issues.

This commitment of SimCorner to align its values to the highest industry standards in both customer care and providing eSIM for travelers, allows the company to expand its operations in the near future as well.

SimCorner Expands New Unlimited Data eSIM Plans to Empower European Travelers

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/8814/251104_def04acfb44bba75_003full.jpg

About SimCorner

Founded in 2012 and serving over 200 countries, SimCorner is a platform that provides SIM cards at airports or other places so its users can have reliable networks. The company helps travelers stay connected through accessible services without any additional hassles.

The global brand has helped over 1 million travelers stay connected across 150+ destinations using SimCorner's SIM and eSIM cards.

For more information about SimCorner's new unlimited Europe eSIM plans, refer to the details below.

Contact Info:

Name: Shahzeb Shaikh

Email: support@simcorner.com

Organization: Simcorner

Website: https://simcorner.com/

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/251104

data.title: Goldman Sachs Lifts South Korea Growth Forecast as Trade Tensions Ease — Market Talk
data.description: Reduced U.S.-China trade tensions prompt Goldman Sachs to raise its 2025 GDP growth forecast for South Korea to 1.1% from 0.7% previously. The export-led economy will get a boost from the world's two largest economies likely seeing improved GDP growth following their May 12 trade deal to roll back tariffs on goods, Goldman Sachs economists Goohoon Kwon and Andrew Tilton write in a note. South Korea's GDP growth is seen to find support in an additional government budget spending bill that was recently approved, as well as possibly more fiscal stimulus later this year, they say. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: African Markets - Factors to watch on May 16
data.description:
The following company announcements, scheduled economic indicators, debt and currency market moves and political events may affect African markets on Friday.

- - - - -

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asian stocks were ending a strong week on a softer note on Friday as the euphoria over U.S.-China trade talks faded, while revived bets for policy easing in the United States sparked a rally in beaten-down bond markets.

WORLD OIL PRICES

Oil prices edged up on Friday following a sharp drop in the previous session, heading for a weekly gain of more than 1% as U.S.-China trade optimism outweighed the prospects of Iranian supply returning to the market.

EMERGING MARKETS

For the top emerging markets news, double click on

AFRICA STOCKS

For the latest news on South African stocks, click on

SOUTH AFRICA MARKETS

South Africa's rand firmed on Thursday, after a deputy finance minister indicated that a lower inflation target could be introduced soon, analysts said.

AFRICA CURRENCIES

The Kenyan and Nigerian currencies are expected to weaken slightly against the dollar in the next week to Thursday, while Uganda's and Zambia's may hold steady and Ghana's could add to recent gains, traders said.

NIGERIA INFLATION

Nigeria's headline inflation rate (NGCPIY=ECI) fell slightly in April, to 23.71% year on year from 24.23% in March, data from its statistics agency showed on Thursday.

KENYA MARKETS

Kenya's shilling was stable on Thursday but market participants said it could weaken slightly later in the session due to potentially higher demand for dollars from general goods importers.

AFRICA EBRD

The European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD) has cleared new member states Nigeria, Ivory Coast and Benin for investments following approval by the development finance institution's board, it said on Thursday.

RWANDA CENTRAL BANK RATE

Rwanda's central bank kept its policy rate unchanged at 6.5% in a decision announced on Thursday and said it planned to start buying gold for its international reserves.

GUINEA MINING

Guinea has revoked the licences of 46 mining companies operating in the country, a government source told Reuters on Thursday, while another said it could be up to 53 permits, in what some analysts see as a warning shot to larger operators in the world's second-largest bauxite producer.

DEMOCRATIC REPUBLIC OF CONGO SECURITY

President Donald Trump's senior adviser for Africa said on Thursday he spoke with the presidents of Rwanda and Democratic Republic of Congo about a draft peace deal this week, as Washington seeks to end a decades-long conflict in the region.

data.title: CJ Cheiljedang's Net Attributable Income Plunges 80.8%
data.description:
South Korean food products manufacturer CJ Cheiljedang posted first-quarter net income attributable to shareholders of the parent company of 17.6 billion won, down 80.8% from 91.9 billion won a year earlier, according to a Wednesday regulatory filing with Korea Exchange.

The company's sales fell 0.1% year over year to 7.208 trillion won from 7.215 trillion won.

Shares of CJ Cheiljedang fell more than 1% in recent trade on Friday.

data.title: SGX Sicom RSS3 Rubber Untraded at Midday
SGX Sicom Benchmark June TSR20 Rubber Down 0.5 Cent at 174.9 U.S. Cents/Kg
ANZ Research Says 'Best-Case Scenario' From US-China Trade Talks Still Likely to Weigh on Global Growth Prospects
data.description:
While the 90-day trade truce between the US and China points to progress, the Trump administration's tariff policy will continue to dent the global outlook, reinforcing the need for central banks to cut rates, ANZ Research said in a Friday note.

With negotiations still ongoing, ANZ Research considers the "best-case scenario" being 30% tariffs on China and 10% on other trading partners. This would leave the average US tariff rate at 18%, which is the highest since 1934, per the report.

ANZ Research also cautioned against abrupt changes to policies associated with the current "uncertainty administration."

The firm expects global gross domestic product growth to soften to 2.9% from 3.2% before the April tariffs. Like other central banks, the Reserve Bank of Australia is expected to ease its policy in response to bleak economic prospects.

ANZ Research anticipates the RBA to deliver three more rate cuts this year.
